preamble: The shift from the voiceless velar fricative [x] to the glottal stop [ʔ] is a form of extreme debuccalization and lenition, typically occurring in word-final positions or as part of a reductive phonetic sequence.

examples.note: In Cusco and Ayacucho Quechuan, the uvular fricative [χ] undergoes debuccalization to [ʔ] in rapid speech and younger speakers under Spanish language contact influence.
Documentation of back-velar or uvular sounds debuccalizing to glottal stops in specific positions.
Occasional replacement of posterior fricatives with glottal stops in rapid speech or as a boundary marker.

sources: Phonological research on Nakh and Mayan consonant reduction.
